sections.paragraph:
ὥρα, ας, ἡ [IE]

—1. ‘a period of time in the day’, hour, time

—a. w. precise period of the day unspecified Mt 24:36; 25:13; Lk 12:39f; Rv 3:3.

—b. w. notation of a specific period of the day

—α.without specific temporal intervals Mt 20:12; 26:40; Mk 11:11; Lk 22:59; J 11:9; Ac 19:34. Cp. in their narrative context Lk 2:38; Ac 16:18; 1 Cor 4:11.

—β. w. specific temporal intervals reckoned from the beginning of the day (this would be the ‘first hour’= approximately our 6 hours or 6 a.m.) or the beginning of the night (= approximately 18 hours or 6 p.m.) hour Mt 20:3, 5,9; 27:45; Mk 15:25; Lk 23:44; J 1:39; 19:14; Ac 2:15; 3:1; 10:30. Water clocks or sundials would contribute to a measure of precision.

—γ. in extended sense of a relatively short period (a/the) time J 5:35; 2 Cor 7:8; 1 Th 2:17. πρὸς ὥραν for a moment Gal 2:5; for a time Phlm 15. μιᾷ ὥρᾳ in a moment Rv 18:10.

—2. ‘a point of time as occasion for action or for an event’, time Mt 8:13; Mk 13:11; 14:35; Lk 10:21; 13:31; J 5:28 and freq. in J; Ac 16:33; Ro 13:11; 1 J 2:18; Rv 11:13; 14:15.
